debug_info.goal:
case refine_1
n : ℕ
S : Error.Dist n
a b : Error.Agent n
σ : List (Error.Call n)
this : @S b = true
c : Error.Agent n
o : Error.maxOne n (Error.Call.normal n a c :: σ)
a_not_know_before_t :
  ¬Error.eval n S ⟨σ, Error.Sequence.maxOne_cons n (Error.Call.normal n a c) σ o⟩
      (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, true)))
a_not_know_before_f :
  ¬Error.eval n S ⟨σ, Error.Sequence.maxOne_cons n (Error.Call.normal n a c) σ o⟩
      (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, false)))
a_knows_after_t : Error.eval n S ⟨Error.Call.normal n a c :: σ, o⟩ (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, true)))
⊢ (b, @S b) ∈ Error.resultSet n a S ⟨σ, Error.resultSet._proof_2 n σ a c o⟩ ∨
    (b, @S b) ∈ Error.resultSet n c S ⟨σ, Error.resultSet._proof_2 n σ a c o⟩

h
              simp [resultSet] at h
              -- If deleted, then a knows it's false in some equivalent world, but a knows it's true!
              -- This requires consistency of knowledge which follows from validity.
              sorry
            · -- (b, false) not in result set
              by_contra h
              -- If (b, false) is in result set, a has a conflict (since a also has (b, true)).

simp [resultSet]; aesop

right
